vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

requirements: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on avionics bay due to high-altitude operation.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management channels and cooling fins.

risk.desc: Camera window seal may degrade over time due to environmental exposure.
risk.mit: Use UV-resistant sealant and regular inspection/maintenance.

risk.desc: Landing gear mount may experience high loads during landing.
risk.mit: Increase material thickness and perform load testing.

risk.desc: Wing spar socket may not provide sufficient clearance for nacelle tilt.
risk.mit: Review and adjust clearance dimensions as necessary.

risk.desc: Potential for service access panel to interfere with avionics bay maintenance.
risk.mit: Optimize panel design for clear access paths.

risk.desc: Potential material fatigue due to high-altitude operations.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal expansion mismatch between CFRP and Al components.
risk.mit: Use thermal expansion coefficients to design joints and interfaces.

risk.desc: Insufficient thermal management might affect servo lifespan in high-altitude missions.
risk.mit: Implement active cooling or larger heat sinks.

risk.desc: Potential for corrosion in saltwater environments due to exposed metal components.

risk.desc: Risk of jamming in extreme cold due to material contraction.
risk.mit: Select materials with low thermal expansion coefficients.

risk.mit: Increase clearance around access points.

risk.desc: Potential for misalignment during assembly due to complex interface fits.
risk.mit: Include detailed assembly instructions and alignment aids.

generated_parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
generated_parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

requirements.description: Provide pitch and yaw control for the tiltrotor VTOL hybrid with a V-tail ruddervator pair.
Operate within the environmental conditions of -20 to +50C, rain, dust, and salt.
Carry loads from the wing spar into the fuselage spine.
Provide access for landing gear maintenance.
Ensure power and signal integrity to the ruddervator servo.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal expansion causing misalignment in high-altitude conditions.
risk.mit: Design with thermal expansion joints and material selection for low coefficient of thermal expansion.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient shock absorption leading to premature wear of landing gear components.
risk.mit: Increase shock absorber stroke and use materials with higher energy absorption.

risk.desc: Risk of improper fastener torque leading to joint failure.
risk.mit: Implement torque verification steps in assembly sequence.

risk.desc: Risk of inadequate clearance for wheel replacement in thin-air conditions.
risk.mit: Design with additional clearance and ergonomic access paths.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure under extreme maneuvering loads.
risk.mit: Conduct load testing and reinforce critical load paths.
